findings: fact-only는 사실의 허용 근거, 추론·가정 금지, 불명확성 하드 스톱, Evidence Labels, Reporting Rule을 정의한다.
agent-rule은 Core Rule과 Evidence-First Workflow에서 같은 근거 유형과 요구사항 발명 금지를 다시 정의한다.
agent-rule은 별도로 Critical Thinking, engineering principles, change safety, runtime restart safety, frontend verification, security boundary, state-model rules를 정의한다.
interpretations: 사실 판단 규칙은 fact-only가 소유하고 agent-rule이 이를 적용하도록 하면 중복 소유를 줄이면서 각 스킬의 목적을 보존할 수 있다.
limitations: 문서 정적 검토이며 실제 모델의 토큰 사용량이나 행동 변화는 측정하지 않았다.

findings: lifecycle은 모든 Agent Factory lifecycle 작업에 fact-only를 사용하도록 라우팅한다.
lifecycle은 수정, 설계, 코드, 리뷰, refactoring, architecture 및 workflow claim 전에 agent-rule을 사용하도록 라우팅한다.
intake, analysis, user-research는 두 스킬을 함께 적용한다.
interview, specification, work-unit-planner는 fact-only 아래 Interview Decision Gate 경로를 참조한다.
interpretations: fact-only 이름과 Interview Decision Gate 경로를 보존하면 기존 직접 참조의 호환성을 유지할 수 있다.
limitations: 동적 문자열로 생성되는 외부 Plugin 소비자 참조는 저장소 검색으로 확인할 수 없다.

findings: 두 스킬 모두 implicit invocation이 허용되어 있다.
fact-only prompt는 사실·해석·Human 미결정을 구분하도록 지시한다.
agent-rule prompt는 편집·설계 전에 명시적이고 증거 기반이며 범위가 정해진 변경을 요구한다.
작업 시작 전 git status --short 출력은 비어 있었다.
interpretations: 메타데이터 설명도 정리된 책임 경계와 맞추는 것이 필요하다.

content.title: OpenAI Codex: Build skills and customization guidance
findings: Codex skills package instructions, resources, and optional scripts for task-specific capabilities and reusable workflows.
Codex uses progressive disclosure: skill metadata is initially visible and the full SKILL.md is loaded only after selection.
OpenAI recommends keeping each skill focused on one job and using concise descriptions with clear scope and boundaries.
OpenAI customization guidance distinguishes durable repository-wide instructions in AGENTS.md from reusable workflows and domain expertise in skills.
limitations: The documentation defines product authoring guidance but does not evaluate Agent Factory fact-only or compare a standalone fact gate against embedding the same rules in lifecycle or AGENTS.md.

content.title: OpenAI: Improving instruction hierarchy in frontier LLMs
findings: OpenAI reports that reliably prioritizing trusted instructions is a core safety and reliability property for agentic systems that consume user, developer, system, and tool content.
The reported failure analysis identifies overly complicated instructions as a cause of instruction-following and hierarchy failures.
The research design favors instruction-following-simple and objectively gradable tasks, supporting a small explicit gate and focused regression tests.
limitations: The study concerns model training and instruction hierarchy rather than Codex skill-directory architecture.
Its results support a clear trusted-evidence gate but do not determine whether that gate should be a standalone skill, AGENTS.md rule, or lifecycle section.

content.title: NIST AI 600-1: Generative AI Profile
findings: NIST identifies confidently stated erroneous or false output as a generative-AI confabulation risk.
NIST recommends reviewing and verifying sources and citations in generative-AI outputs.
NIST recommends documenting assumptions, limitations, intended context of use, data origin, and content lineage.
These controls support retaining an explicit evidence and uncertainty boundary in Agent Factory.
limitations: NIST provides risk-management outcomes rather than Codex prompt or skill packaging guidance.
The profile supports the fact-boundary behavior but does not support a specific standalone fact-only skill directory.

content.title: When Instructions Multiply: Measuring and Estimating LLM Capabilities of Multiple Instructions Following
findings: The peer-reviewed study evaluates ten language models on multiple simultaneous instructions.
Instruction-following performance consistently degrades as the number of instructions increases.
This supports removing duplicated fact-boundary instructions from fact-only, agent-rule, and adjacent skills rather than repeating them for emphasis.
limitations: The benchmark covers instruction count, not semantically identical duplication or the Agent Factory skill loader specifically.
It supports reducing prompt burden but does not by itself choose between one standalone skill and one embedded lifecycle rule.
